title: Supervised lighting dispatcher: day/dusk/night specialist field heads replace the two-expert blend, int8-paid
hypothesis: The last four reverted experiments (26-29) all attacked capacity or readout width and were jointly refuted; per the plateau rule this iteration leaves that family entirely and opens the one design family CLAUDE.md §3 explicitly invites but the history has never tried: dispatcher + lighting-condition specialist models. The evidence it targets: the champion's per-bucket medians LOOK flat (berlin 632-760 m), but that flatness is manufactured by the confidence gate — night keeps only 25-31% of crops vs 50-67% for daytime buckets, so the model abstains its way to parity in low light instead of localizing to parity, and the binding §6 cell is a low-ambient one (frankfurt EVENING, 797.91 m at 0.46 coverage). The mechanism: all six lighting regimes share ONE 560→1024 template table, plus exp 12's deliberately crippled texture-only 'dark expert' (48-d input, no layout access, unsupervised gate). The same map cell must therefore be recognized by one template averaged across appearance regimes with nearly disjoint cues — daytime shadow/texture vs night's active-light constellations — so every template is a compromise, worst exactly where cues are scarcest. Exp 12 is direct evidence the axis works: even its crippled version was kept (-8%). This experiment builds the full version: each regime gets its own complete layout-aware 560→1024 template table, routing is SUPERVISED (bucket labels are free at train time), and the 3x table storage is paid in int8 FC bytes, which exps 26/29 mechanically proved passes the frozen gates at ~7 m accuracy cost.
method: model/model.py: delete dark_logits and the sigmoid gate; replace loc_logits with three identical specialist heads Linear(560→1024) in an nn.ModuleList (day/dusk/night) and add a 3-way dispatcher MLP Linear(49→16)-ReLU-Linear(16→3) reading [lum, GAP]; per turned copy, field logits = Σ_k softmax(dispatcher)_k · specialist_k(desc); C4 vote, decode, confidence all unchanged. loss_fn gains a supervised routing term: 0.3 × cross-entropy of the dispatcher logits vs the crop's regime (morning/midday/afternoon→day, early_evening/evening→dusk, night→night). model/train.py: sample_epoch also returns the per-crop regime index; the train step passes it to loss_fn. export_onnx: fp32 export to <area>_fp32_debug.onnx, then onnxruntime.quantization.quantize_dynamic(QInt8, op_types=['MatMul','Gemm']) to the contract path — the exact recipe exps 26/29 validated (convs stay fp32; no ConvInteger); assert size ≤ 4 MiB.
expected_outcome: If the lighting-shared readout is binding as hypothesized: night and evening cells improve most, night coverage rises above its current 0.25-0.31 at the unchanged 40% calibration floor, and the primary worst-case drops from 797.91 m to ~700-760 m, with the binding cell either shrinking in place (frankfurt evening) or migrating to a daytime bucket. Prignitz should stay the best area. Clean refutation: a statistical tie (±3%) with healthy routing (train-split dispatcher accuracy >95%) would show regime-pure templates add nothing over exp 12's blend — a different refutation than the capacity family (26-29), since parameters per APPLIED readout are unchanged here; only regime-conditioning is added. Confound pre-registered: the known coverage-median coupling — a median win bought by a coverage drop in the same cells is not a win; the per-bucket coverage log discriminates.
init_strategy: pretrained:mobilenet_v3_small IMAGENET1K_V1 features[0..8] (torchvision, BSD-3) — trunk unchanged; the three specialist heads and the dispatcher are fresh-initialized
eli5: The model recognizes places by comparing what it sees against a big internal catalog of how every map cell looks. Until now one shared catalog had to describe each place at noon AND at midnight — like one photo album trying to cover both — so every entry was a blurry compromise, and the model coped in the dark mostly by refusing to answer. We split it into three catalogs — day, dusk, night — and added a tiny receptionist that checks how bright the frame is and hands the image to the right catalog. During practice the receptionist is simply told the real time of day, so it can't learn the routing wrong. Three catalogs would normally be too heavy for the drone's tiny computer, so they're stored in a compact 1-byte number format we measured earlier to be essentially harmless.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: Rotation fan
stages.detail: the crop is copied into its four 90° turns (exact pixel re-arrangements, no blur) and all four copies travel through the same network side by side

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small (first 9 blocks, 190k params), gently fine-tuned

stages.name: Layout summary
stages.detail: a slim extra layer condenses the 8×8 feature grid into a 512-number code that remembers what sits where in the crop; the texture average (48 numbers) is kept alongside

stages.name: Lighting dispatcher
stages.detail: REPLACES the old lighting gate: instead of one 'how night-like?' number blending two scorers, a tiny receptionist network reads the crop's brightness and its 48-number texture summary and outputs three routing weights — how day-like, dusk-like and night-like the shot is. During training it is told the true time of day, so the routing is learned supervised instead of guessed

stages.name: Probability map
stages.detail: three FULL place-scorers — day, dusk and night specialists, each with the complete what-sits-where readout — score every map cell, and their per-cell guesses are mixed using the dispatcher's three weights; the four turned copies are still averaged into one voted heat map. The old design was one shared full scorer plus a texture-only night helper; now each lighting regime owns its whole catalog, stored as compact 1-byte integers so three catalogs still fit the flight computer (a storage trick measured beforehand to cost only ~7 m)

stages.name: Decode
stages.detail: the heat map's contrast is turned up first (peaks boosted, faint background suppressed), then the answer is the balance point of the sharpened map — committing to the strongest hotspot instead of averaging every faint guess across the whole city

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: the model inspects the shape of its own heat map and says 'no fix' below a tuned bar — tuned as before on places it visited during training

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: one NEW grading: the dispatcher's routing is graded against the true lighting condition of each training crop (known for free in the simulator, weight 0.3); position and confidence grading unchanged

stages.name: Training data
stages.detail: unchanged — a fresh 6,000-place draw per lighting condition every pass, each place seen at a fresh heading and one of three relight re-renders

stages.name: Training schedule
stages.detail: unchanged — 24 fresh-draw passes with the cosine glide kept from round 5

implementation_brief: Edit model/model.py and model/train.py ONLY; pipeline/* and every file in /FROZEN stay byte-identical; never touch or read the hamburg area. FIXED CONTRACTS TO PRESERVE: train.py's CLI (--area --out-dir --data-dir --epochs --max-crops-per-bucket --seed, same defaults) and its output flow (train_info.json append, models/<area>.onnx); the ONNX contract from model/model.py's docstring — one export per area at <out>/models/<area>.onnx taking a 1x3x128x128 float32 input in [0,1] named 'frame' and returning [[u, v, conf]] with u,v normalized map coords and conf in [0,1] — and the frozen deployment gates in pipeline/score.py (file <= 4 MiB, host latency proxy <= 250 ms). build_model(), estimate_position, GRID_K=32, TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS, DECODE_BETA, GOOD_ERR_UV, _c4_stack's rotation-major order, the pretrained trunk load (mnv3s_features8.pt, strict=True), the conf head, conf_shift calibration flow, EPOCH_MULT=3, TRAIN_REALIZATIONS=3, and the fresh-draw sampler all stay exactly as they are. CHANGE 1 — model/model.py module level: add N_REGIMES = 3, DISPATCH_CE_W = 0.3, and REGIMES = {'morning': 0, 'midday': 0, 'afternoon': 0, 'early_evening': 1, 'evening': 1, 'night': 2} with a one-line comment (keys must match pipeline.common.LIGHTING_BUCKETS; 0=day, 1=dusk, 2=night). CHANGE 2 — TinyLocNet.__init__: DELETE self.dark_logits and self.gate. REPLACE self.loc_logits with self.specialists = nn.ModuleList([nn.Linear(feat_ch + layout_ch * fmap_hw * fmap_hw, grid_k * grid_k) for _ in range(N_REGIMES)]). ADD self.dispatcher = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(feat_ch + 1, gate_hidden), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(gate_hidden, N_REGIMES)) (keep the gate_hidden=16 ctor arg). layout_squeeze, conf_head, conf_shift, cell_u/cell_v, norm buffers unchanged. CHANGE 3 — TinyLocNet.forward: keep lum, x4, fmap, f4, layout4, lum4 exactly as now. Then: desc4 = torch.cat([f4, layout4], dim=1); disp_logits4 = self.dispatcher(torch.cat([lum4, f4], dim=1))  # [4N, 3]; w4 = torch.softmax(disp_logits4, dim=1); logits4 = sum(w4[:, k:k+1] * self.specialists[k](desc4) for k in range(N_REGIMES)). Everything after logits4 — the C4 reshape/mean into logits, f, both softmax decodes, the detached conf features, conf head, conf_shift, output cat — is byte-identical. Return contract: default forward(x) returns out ONLY (ONNX export depends on this); forward(x, return_logits=True) now returns (out, logits, disp_logits4) — note logits is the C4-averaged [N, K*K] tensor as before while disp_logits4 stays [4N, 3]. CHANGE 4 — loss_fn: new signature loss_fn(pred, logits, target_uv, disp_logits4=None, regime=None). Existing CE + coord + conf terms byte-identical. If disp_logits4 is not None: regime4 = regime.repeat(4) (correct for _c4_stack's rotation-major torch.cat order — the batch repeats 4x) and add DISPATCH_CE_W * torch.nn.functional.cross_entropy(disp_logits4, regime4). CHANGE 5 — export_onnx(model, path): keep the signature. p = Path(path); fp32_path = p.with_name(p.stem + '_fp32_debug.onnx'); run the existing torch.onnx.export call to fp32_path; then from onnxruntime.quantization import quantize_dynamic, QuantType; quantize_dynamic(fp32_path, path, weight_type=QuantType.QInt8, op_types_to_quantize=['MatMul', 'Gemm']) — the exact recipe runs/_probe_iter6_quant2.py validated and exps 26/29 shipped (convs stay fp32; ~7 m decode shift). Afterwards onnx.load(path) and raise RuntimeError if any node.op_type == 'ConvInteger' (the probe-documented fatal op) or Path(path).stat().st_size > 4*1024*1024; never fall back to shipping the fp32 file (~7.8 MB, fails the frozen gate). Leave the _fp32_debug.onnx in <out>/models/ for forensics (precedent exp 26; pipeline/score.py reads only <area>.onnx). CHANGE 6 — model/model.py docstring: append a short 'Exp 30' paragraph describing the supervised dispatcher + three int8-paid specialists; keep all existing contract wording intact. CHANGE 7 — model/train.py: from model.model import REGIMES (alongside the existing imports). In sample_epoch, add rs = [] parallel to xs/ys and inside the inner per-crop loop append REGIMES[bucket] in the SAME iteration order as xs.append/ys.append; return (x, y, torch.tensor(rs, dtype=torch.long)). In train_area's epoch loop: x, y, r = sample_epoch(...); inside the minibatch loop rb = r[idx].to(device); out, logits, disp4 = model(xb, return_logits=True); loss = loss_fn(out, logits, yb, disp4, rb). Keep the existing del x, y and add r to it. calibrate_conf_shift is untouched (it calls model(xb) on the default single-return path). In the info dict update 'init' to note the fresh specialist heads + dispatcher and add 'dispatch_ce_weight': 0.3 and 'regimes': 'day{morning,midday,afternoon} dusk{early_evening,evening} night{night}'. SANITY NUMBERS: total params ~1.92M (specialists 3 x 574,464 = 1,723,392; dispatcher 851; trunk 927,008 bytes of that is untouched fp32); fp32 debug export ~7.7-7.8 MB; quantized export ~2.5-2.7 MB (well under the 4 MiB gate); latency: two EXTRA 560x1024 Gemms per turned copy are negligible (probe measured 4.6 ms on a bigger int8 model; gate is 250 ms). Training-side check: all new params (specialists, dispatcher) are NOT under model.features, so they land in train.py's existing 1e-3 'head' param group automatically — verify, do not restructure the optimizer. Expect the dispatcher CE to collapse toward 0 within the first epochs (brightness makes the regime nearly linearly separable) — that is intended, not a bug.
